This means the macOS traffic lights (red/yellow/green window controls) are positioned at top-left (16, 16). So I need to:

2. Rework the layout - maybe move the logo and name to center or right

For the name, thinking of something "non obvious but obvious and cool" like the example "nightmarket" -> "agentic service marketplace". Let me think:
- "Command Center" could be something like "Mission Control", "Nexus", "Hub", "Bridge"
- Agent-related: "Swarm", "Hive", "Colony", "Orchestra"
- Cool names: "Cortex" (brain center), "Synapse" (neural connection), "Nexus" (connection point), "Hive" (collective intelligence)

Some options:
- "The Hive" - collective intelligence, agents working together
- "Nexus" - connection point for all agents
- "Cortex" - the brain/control center
- "Orchestra" - coordinating multiple agents
- "Swarm" - distributed agent system
- "Bridge" - like Star Trek's command center
- "Dispatch" - sending agents on missions

I like "Dispatch" - it's short, suggests sending agents out to complete tasks, has a control-room vibe, and is not too obvious but makes sense when you think about it.


- It conveys the core function of coordinating and directing agents to their destinations
- Punchy and modern sounding
- Immediately clicks when you understand what the platform does

file.content:
# Agent Command Center

Orchestrate AI coding agents from a unified interface. Built around four pillars: **Task Alignment**, **Steerability**, **Verifiability**, and **Adaptability**.

## Vision

An open-source Electron app that lets you orchestrate multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, OpenClaw, etc.) with:

- **Planning View**: Describe tasks, see breakdown, confirm before execution
- **Execution View**: TMux-style widget grid for real-time visibility
- **Memory System**: Org-level + project-level knowledge (markdown files)
- **Integrations**: GitHub, CodeRabbit CLI

## Quick Start

```bash
# Install dependencies
bun install

# Build contracts
cd packages/contracts && bun run build

# Start server (port 3333)
cd packages/server && bun run.ts

# Start UI (separate terminal)
cd packages/ui && bun dev
```

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│         Electron App (UI)           │
│  React 19 + Tailwind + Vite         │
└────────────────┬────────────────────┘
                 │ IPC / WebSocket
                 ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│       Local Companion Server        │
│  Hono HTTP + WebSocket (port 3333)  │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Adapters:                          │
│  - Claude Code (subprocess, -p)     │
│  - OpenClaw (WebSocket + cron API)  │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Integrations:                      │
│  - GitHub CLI (gh pr, gh issue)     │
│  - CodeRabbit CLI (cr --prompt-only)│
└─────────────────────────────────────┘

{
  "name": "@acc/ui",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "main": "dist-electron/main.js",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "bun run --parallel dev:vite dev:electron",
    "dev:vite": "vite",
    "dev:electron": "bun run scripts/dev-electron.mjs",
    "build": "vite build && bun run build:electron",
    "build:electron": "electron-builder",
    "start": "electron .",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "lint": "eslint src/"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@acc/contracts": "workspace:*",
    "@xterm/xterm": "^5.5.0",
    "clsx": "^2.1.0",
    "lucide-react": "^0.577.0",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "react-markdown": "^10.1.0",
    "react-resizable-panels": "^2.1.0",
    "remark-gfm": "^4.0.1",
    "shiki": "^4.0.2",
    "tailwind-merge": "^2.6.0",
    "zustand": "^5.0.11"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@tailwindcss/vite": "^4.0.0",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.0",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.4.0",
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.20",
    "electron": "^40.0.0",
    "electron-builder": "^25.0.0",
    "postcss": "^8.5.0",
    "tailwindcss": "^4.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.8.0",
    "vite": "^6.2.0"
  },
  "build": {
    "appId": "com.agentcommandcenter.app",
    "productName": "Agent Command Center",
    "directories": {
      "output": "release"
    },
    "files": [
      "dist/**/*",
      "dist-electron/**/*"
    ],
    "mac": {
      "target": ["dmg", "zip"],
      "category": "public.app-category.developer-tools"
    },
    "win": {
      "target": ["nsis", "zip"]
    },
    "linux": {
      "target": ["AppImage", "deb"]
    }
  }
}

content.thinking: Perfect! Now let me create a summary of the changes I made for the user.

content.text:
Perfect! I've reworked the title bar to work properly with macOS window controls. Here's what I changed:

## Layout Changes

**Header Bar (Workspace.tsx:1271-1344)**:
- Added 80px left spacer to avoid overlap with macOS traffic lights (red/yellow/green buttons)
- Centered the logo (🦞) and app name in the middle
- Moved workspace path selector to center section
- Kept action buttons on the right
- Made the header draggable (with `drag-region` class) while keeping buttons clickable (`no-drag` class)

## Rebranding to "Dispatch"

Changed from "Agent Command Center" / "ACC" to **"Dispatch"**:
- **Why "Dispatch"?** Like your "nightmarket" example, it's "non-obvious but obvious and cool" - suggests coordinating and sending agents on missions, has that operations/control-room vibe
- Updated app title in header
- Updated HTML page title
- Updated Electron productName and appId in package.json

The new layout ensures nothing overlaps with the macOS window controls, and "Dispatch" gives it a more focused, modern identity!

message.content: instead of a lobster icon, use something that makes more sense

content.thinking:
The user wants me to replace the lobster emoji (🦞) with something that makes more sense for "Dispatch". Let me think about what icons would make sense for an app called "Dispatch" that coordinates AI agents:

- 📡 Satellite/radio tower - suggests dispatching/broadcasting
- 🎯 Target - suggests precision/directing
- ⚡ Lightning bolt - fast action
- 🚀 Rocket - sending things out
- 📢 Megaphone - dispatching messages
- 🗂️ File cabinet - organization
- 📊 Chart - command center
- 🎛️ Control panel - command center vibe
- 🔀 Shuffle/routing - routing agents
- 🧭 Compass - navigation/direction
- ⚙️ Gear - operations
- 🌐 Globe - network
- 📶 Signal bars - dispatch/communication
- 🛰️ Satellite - communication hub
- 📋 Clipboard - task management

For "Dispatch" specifically, I think the best options are:
- 📡 Satellite dish - suggests broadcast/communication/coordination
- 🎯 Target - precision task execution
- 📋 Clipboard - dispatching tasks/orders
- 🧭 Compass - directing/navigating agents

I'll suggest a few options and let the user choose, or I could just pick one. Let me go with 📡 as it best captures the "dispatch/coordination center" concept.

Looking at the changes and the commit message style:
- Changed "Agent Command Center" to "Dispatch" across multiple files
- Updated the header layout to avoid overlapping with macOS traffic lights
- Changed icon from lobster to satellite dish
- Made header draggable with proper no-drag zones for interactive elements

The commit message should be:
- Type: This is a rebrand + UI improvement, so I'd use "feat" for the new branding
- Scope: (ui) since it's UI-related
- Message: Should describe both the rebrand to Dispatch and the macOS-friendly header layout

content.text:
Perfect! I've committed the changes with the message:

```
feat(ui): rebrand to Dispatch and fix macOS title bar layout

Rebrand from "Agent Command Center" to "Dispatch" with satellite dish icon. Redesign header with centered title and proper spacing for macOS traffic lights to prevent overlap.
```

The commit includes:
- Rebranding to "Dispatch" (HTML title, package.json productName/appId)
- New 📡 satellite dish icon replacing the lobster
- Header layout redesigned with centered title and 80px left spacing for macOS window controls
- Proper drag region setup with no-drag zones for interactive elements
